summary: Recent updates to the Media Source Extensions (MSE) in Firefox focused on enhancing video playback capabilities, particularly for formats like H.264 and HEVC. A notable addition is the **new testing framework for HEVC (High Efficiency Video Coding) without a sequence parameter set (SPS), which simplifies the way video files can be processed**. This is important because it ensures better compatibility and performance when streaming videos in these formats. Alongside this, tests for H.264 with oversized picture widths were introduced, helping developers ensure that video content can be handled efficiently, even in edge cases. Overall, these improvements lead to a more robust media playback experience in the browser.
